parsed_review.summary: CP00-454 is a descriptor-only RP15 "Firm Analytics" closeout pack (Risk C, 150 units, RP15.P01.M06.S01-RP15.P02.M04.S06) that closes the P01 model-foundation phase and opens the P02 foundation as ten descriptor-only sections. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE (command-evidence.json and manifest.json), with status pending final human closeout; it does not over-claim enterprise trust. All listed validation commands (node --check x5, node --test, every rpNN:*:validate, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/fullplan/goal/control-plane validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check) recorded exit_code 0. Coverage and distributions reconcile: deliverable_distribution {ui:23,implementation:77,fixture:3,test:17,hermes_evidence:3,claude_review:6,contract:5,security_audit:10,failure_recovery:6} and micro_title_distribution both sum to 150 and were hand-verified against closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-454 units and confirmed by passing validators/tests. The analytics-core contract deep-equals the in-code pack binding, requirements, no-write attestation, and embedded p01_closeout_p02_foundation_descriptor; historical_pack_bindings ends at CP00-454. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: every freeze/attestation flag is false, all objects are frozen, and no writeFile/fetch/exec/network calls exist in packages/analytics or the validator. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (claude_is_final_approval:false; review-request read_only with Read/Grep/Glob only) and is not final approver; human approval is required and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns appear only as gate labels (H15) and descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), never product runtime. Review evidence is valid and hardened: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte non-empty valid-JSON stdout), validation-report confirms prompt size ok, read-tools inspection mode, no forbidden tools, schema present, and summary-only correctly prohibited. Public exports resolve via index.js re-export (passing tests). Only two non-blocking P3 cosmetic notes; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Note: post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are not yet present, which is expected since this review produces their input; they must exist and pass the post-review gated commands before commit.

findings.title: Cosmetic: test case title references RP14 instead of RP15
findings.details: The test title "CP00-454 plan binding covers the planned 150 RP14 p01 closeout p02 foundation units" says RP14, but CP00-454 is an RP15 (Firm Analytics) pack whose upstream PROGRAM is RP14. All assertions inside the test correctly verify RP15 program/pack values, so this is a label-only inaccuracy with no functional effect.
findings.recommendation: Change "RP14" to "RP15" in the test description string for accuracy. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Open cosmetic nit recorded for the human reviewer; does not gate CP00-454 closeout.

findings.title: By-design: CP454 ROW_EXTRAS reuses CP453 extras, so P02 service/permission/audit rows carry only base descriptor guards
findings.details: ANALYTICS_CORE_CP454_ROW_EXTRAS spreads CP453's extras only, so new RP15.P02 row keys (service_entrypoint_contract, tenant_boundary_precheck, permission_precheck, audit_hint_precheck, etc.) get no row-specific extras and are validated only by the four base checks (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false). This is acceptable for a descriptor-only pack because the pack-level no_write_attestation already asserts evaluates_runtime_permission=false and writes_audit_event=false and the rows carry no permission/audit payload; the deeper per-field guards in CP453 applied to specifically-named scope-foundation rows. No leak or write risk is introduced.
findings.recommendation: No action required. If future packs open P02 runtime, add row-specific permission/audit guards (as CP453 did for permission_baseline_note/audit_baseline_note) at that point. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational/by-design observation for the human reviewer; does not gate CP00-454 closeout.
